Effect: +1 to d6 rolls for wilderness survival, tracking, and stealth in outdoor environments. Critical hit range expands to 19-20 against beasts.
Notes: Any class. Works well as a Hunter or Delver variant foundation.

Effect: +1 to melee ATK and DEF when wielding a one-handed weapon.
Notes: A Caster with Blade Dancer can hold their own in melee without spending spell slots.

Effect: Free counterattack on a natural 19-20 on a defense roll when wielding a single one-handed sword or sword and dagger.
Notes: Expands the natural 20 counterattack window. Pairs well with Delver sneak attack.

Effect: +1 ATK with light weapons. +1 to d6 rolls for sailing, sea lore, and open-water survival.
Heal 1d6 + RES modifier HP on touch once per day. +1 to d6 rolls for religious lore, ceremony, and faith-based persuasion.
Notes: A Hunter with this talent is a paladin. A Caster with this talent is a priest who also casts spells.

Effect: Curse once per day: one target suffers -1 to all rolls until they rest (WIS save target 13 resists). +1 to d6 rolls for occult lore and curse identification.
Speak with animals at will (simple communication; animals not compelled). Commune with nature once per day: ask the referee one question about the local natural environment.
+1 ATK and damage with chosen ranged weapon type (bow; firearm; or sling; chosen at creation).
Stealth attacks with ranged weapons: +4 ATK and sneak attack multiplier applies (Delver only) or max damage (any class). Can engage at extreme range at referee discretion.
Notes: Delvers use their class sneak multiplier. Other classes get max weapon damage only, no multiplier.

Effect: On successful defense roll of natural 19-20 vs small thrown weapon: catch and return it (up to 3/round). Once per day reroll any single die that affects you.
Roll defense for an adjacent willing ally once per round in addition to your own defense roll.
Notes: Requires being adjacent to the protected ally.

Effect: When you take damage that reduces you below half HP, gain +2 ATK until end of combat.
Notes: Activates once per combat when threshold is crossed.
Choosing a magic tradition is a talent. Casters only. Taken at character creation or through significant in-world training.

Effect: CHA replaces WIS for all spell-related rolls. Different spell list emphasizing nature, spirits, elemental effects, and communal magic. Learns new spells by communing with spirits rather than research. Cannot use scrolls.
Notes: Shaman is a Caster tradition not a separate class. The spirit does the casting; the Shaman negotiates.

Effect: Spells stored in gadgets rather than cast directly. Each gadget holds one prepared spell with charges equal to level divided by spell tier (min 1). Anyone can activate a gadget without declaration or slot cost to the activator. Gadgets cannot be swapped mid-expedition.
Notes: Key tradeoff: Artificer avoids all casting interruption risk but carries more items. Losing a gadget is manageable; losing all gadgets is catastrophic.
